name: NGINX Baseline — API Rate Limit
objective: Provide an NGINX rate-limit baseline for API endpoints that need burst control and basic abuse throttling at the reverse proxy layer.
risk_mitigated: Automated abuse, token guessing, enumeration, and burst traffic against API authentication and query endpoints.
potential_side_effects: Partner integrations or mobile traffic behind shared carrier NAT may need per-route burst adjustments.
tuning_notes: Create separate zones for login, token, and general API traffic. Use lower burst allowances for unauthenticated routes than for trusted authenticated APIs.
deployment_notes: Apply first to staging ingress or a canary pool and validate legitimate client retry behavior before broad rollout.
monitoring_notes: Monitor 429 volume, source IP concentration, and which endpoints trigger the limit most frequently. Correlate with application auth failures when possible.
